background: Assume there are 10 identical units to place on test at the same time t = 0 where each unit has an exponential life distribution with a mean time to failure MTTF μ = 5 h and that the units operate independently.

task.query: Determine the expected and the variance of number of failures during the first hour, i.e. interval [0, 1).
task.answer: Let $N_1$ be the number of failures during the first hour, in the time interval $[0$, 1). The probability of failure of a unit in the time interval $[0,1)$ is $$ q_1=1-e^{- rac{1}{5}}=0.1813 $$ The distribution of $N_1$ that the probability of exactly $i$ failures in $[0,1)$ is $$ P\left(N_1=i\right)=\binom{10}{i}(0.1813)^i(0.8187)^{10-i} $$ The expected number of failures $\left(N_1\right)$ during the first hour is $$ E\left(N_1\right)=n q_1=10(0.1813)=1.813 $$ The variance of $N_1$ is $$ \begin{aligned} V\left(N_1\right) & =n q_1\left(1-q_1\right) \\ & =10(0.1813)(0.8187)=1.4843 \end{aligned} $$

task.query: What is the expected and the variance of the number of failures during the interval [1, 2).
task.answer: Let $N_2$ be the number of failures during the second hour, in the time interval $[1,2)$. Assume that the unit will not fail during the first hour, the probability of failure of a unit in the time interval $[1,2)$ is $$ \begin{aligned} q_2 & =P(1<T \leq 2)=P(T \leq 2)-P(T \leq 1) \\ & =\left(1-e^{-\frac{2}{5}}\right)-\left(1-e^{-\frac{1}{5}}\right)=0.1484 \end{aligned} $$ The distribution of $N_2$ that the probability of exactly $i$ failures during the time interval $[1,2)$ is $$ P\left(N_2=i\right)=\binom{10}{i}(0.1484)^i(0.8516)^{10-i} $$ The expected number of failures $\left(N_2\right)$ during the interval $[1,2)$ is $$ E\left(N_2\right)=n q_2=10(0.1484)=1.484 $$ The variance of $\mathrm{N}_2$ is $$ \begin{aligned} V\left(N_2\right) & =n q_2\left(1-q_2\right) \\ & =10(0.1484)(0.8516)=1.2638 \end{aligned} $$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
